evals.prompt: I'm building for Solana Frontier and want to check if my idea is novel. The organizer gave me this URL: https://demo.dstack-pha-prod5.phala.network and instance ID 8ed7d18f-fdda-430d-843e-c8b42a92cdb0. My project is in this directory.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill triggers, surfaces the privacy boundary statement, hits /health and /instances/{id}, displays the TDX attestation, mints a token, summarizes the local repo, asks for confirmation, submits, and persists the submission_id to ~/.conclave/credentials.json.
Skill triggers; if no enclave is configured in ~/.conclave/credentials.json, asks the user for the enclave URL and instance ID before doing anything else. Does not invent a URL.

If a submission_id exists in credentials.json, fetches /results/{id} and renders the participant view (novelty_score, cluster, track_alignments, name_collisions). If no submission_id exists, prompts the user to submit first.

evals.prompt: Verify the Conclave enclave is real.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill calls /attestation and /attestations; shows the TDX quote prefix + verify URL, plus any Solana devnet attestation records with the explorer link; explains the difference between hardware attestation and on-chain attestation in one sentence.

evals.prompt: Can I update my Conclave submission?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill explains that resubmitting under the same token replaces the existing submission (cohort N does not grow), then offers to walk through the submit flow again.

evals.prompt: What does Conclave see about my code?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill states the privacy boundary verbatim from references/privacy.md — local agent reads the repo, only the summary leaves the machine, operator never sees raw idea/code/identity.

evals.prompt: Refactor this React component for me.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill does NOT trigger. This is unrelated to Conclave / hackathon novelty / enclave evaluation.

evals.prompt: Score this PR for novelty against past commits.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill does NOT trigger. This is general novelty assessment, not a hackathon submission to a Conclave enclave.
